name: Pelvic Curl

breathShort.action: Draw the navel down and curl the tailbone up, peeling the spine off the mat one vertebra at a time.
Hold the bridge, lengthening the front of the hips and keeping the ribs soft.
Melt the spine back down from the chest to the tailbone, returning to a neutral pelvis.

topCues: Initiate from the deep abdominals and pelvic floor, not by pushing through the feet.
Keep the weight even across both feet with the knees parallel.
Articulate slowly; feel each vertebra meet and leave the mat.

name: Chest Lift

breathShort.action: Nod the chin gently and curl the head and shoulders up, sliding the ribs toward the pelvis.
Stay lifted and widen the back of the ribs, keeping the abdominals scooped.
Deepen the curl slightly, then lower the spine sequentially back to the mat.

topCues: Lead with the breastbone, not the chin; keep space under the jaw.
Anchor the pelvis in neutral and let the abdominals, not the hip flexors, do the work.
Keep the elbows wide and the neck long and relaxed.

name: Leg Lift Supine

breathShort.action: Stabilize the pelvis and float one leg up to tabletop without rocking the hips.
Lower the leg back to the mat with control, keeping the low back quiet.

topCues: Keep both hip bones level; the pelvis should not tip toward the moving leg.
Move from the hip joint while the torso stays completely still.
Hold a steady connection through the deep abdominals throughout.

name: Leg Lift Side

breathShort.action: Lift the top leg toward the ceiling, keeping the pelvis stacked and still.
Lower the leg slowly back down with the same control.

topCues: Keep the waist long; don't let the underside collapse into the mat.
Lead with the inner thigh and keep the toes facing forward.
Stabilize through the supporting arm and the deep core.

name: Leg Pull Side

breathShort.action: Sweep the top leg forward, keeping the torso steady and the spine long.
Carry the leg back behind the line of the body, opening the front of the hip.

topCues: Move only as far as you can without letting the pelvis rock.
Keep the abdominals lifted so the low back stays stable.
Glide the leg at hip height: control over range.

name: Spine Twist Supine

breathShort.action: Rotate the knees toward one side while keeping the opposite shoulder grounded.
Use the waist to draw the knees back through center.
Rotate to the other side, moving from the obliques, not momentum.
Return to center with length through the spine.
Control the final return and reset the pelvis to neutral.

topCues: Keep both shoulder blades anchored to the mat the whole time.
Turn from the waist, not by swinging the legs.
Keep the knees and ankles together to challenge the obliques.

name: Chest Lift With Rotation

breathShort.action: Curl the head and chest up, drawing the ribs toward the pelvis.
Stay lifted and prepare to rotate, widening across the back.
Rotate the upper torso toward one knee, reaching the opposite shoulder across.
Return through center, keeping the curl high.
Rotate to the other side from the waist.
Return to center and lower with control.

topCues: Rotate the whole rib cage, not just the elbow.
Keep the pelvis still and square as you turn.
Maintain the height of the chest curl throughout the rotations.

name: Back Extension Prone

breathShort.action: Draw the abdominals up off the mat and lengthen the chest forward and up into a low extension.
Lower the breastbone back to the mat with control, restoring length.

topCues: Keep the gaze down and the back of the neck long.
Lift from the upper back, not by crunching the lower back.
Keep the legs active and the pubic bone connected to the mat.

name: One-Leg Circle

breathShort.action: Cross the lifted leg across the body to begin the circle.
Sweep the leg around and up to complete the circle back to the start.

topCues: Keep the pelvis absolutely still; only the leg moves.
Draw the circle from the hip joint, controlled and even.
Anchor the opposite leg and both shoulders into the mat.

name: Roll-Up

breathShort.action: Lying long, reach the arms overhead and begin to lift the head and arms.
Peel the spine off the mat one bone at a time, reaching forward over the legs.
Begin to roll back, scooping the abdominals to initiate.
Restack each vertebra downward until the whole back rests on the mat.

topCues: Move sequentially: no flat-back hinging or yanking up.
Keep the heels down and the shoulders away from the ears.
Use the breath to deepen the abdominal scoop on each curl.

name: Neck Pull

breathShort.action: Hands behind the head, lengthen the spine and begin to curl the head up.
Roll up and forward over the legs, keeping the elbows wide.
Restack the spine to sit tall, hinging back slightly.
Lower through the spine to the floor, one vertebra at a time.

topCues: Keep the elbows in your peripheral vision; don't pull on the neck.
Press the legs down and together for stability.
Articulate slowly; the hands rest, they do not pull.

name: Hundred

breathShort.action: Curl the head and chest up, extend the legs, and begin pumping the arms.
Pump the arms briskly for five beats as you breathe in.
Pump for five beats as you breathe out, keeping the torso lifted and still.

topCues: Keep the lower back imprinted; raise the legs higher if it strains.
Pump from the shoulders with straight arms, palms down.
Hold the chest high with the gaze toward the navel for the full set.

name: One-Leg Stretch

breathShort.action: Curl up, hug one knee to the chest, and extend the other leg long.
Switch legs, drawing the opposite knee in as you breathe out.

topCues: Hold the chest high and the back steady as the legs switch.
Extend the long leg only as low as the back can stay anchored.
Keep the movement smooth and the shoulders relaxed.

name: Single Straight-Leg Stretch

breathShort.action: Curl up and reach one straight leg toward you, gently pulsing it twice.
Switch the legs through center in the air.
Draw the other straight leg in with a light double pulse.

topCues: Keep both legs straight and switch from the hips.
Hold the ankle or calf, never behind the knee.
Maintain a steady chest curl and quiet pelvis.

name: Double-Leg Stretch

breathShort.action: Curl up holding the knees, then reach the arms and legs long in opposite directions.
Circle the arms around and draw the knees back in to the start.

topCues: Extend only as far as you can keep the low back anchored.
Keep the head and chest lifted as the limbs reach away.
Move with control; the abdominals power the return.

name: Crisscross

breathShort.action: Curl up, hands behind the head, and rotate to bring one shoulder toward the opposite knee.
Pass through center, switching the legs.
Rotate to the other side, reaching the shoulder past the opposite knee.

topCues: Rotate the rib cage toward the bent knee, not just the elbow.
Keep the extended leg low and steady, pelvis still.
Keep the elbows wide and the neck long.

name: Teaser

breathShort.action: Float the legs to a low diagonal and begin to peel the torso up off the mat.
Roll up to balance in a V-shape, then articulate the spine back down with control.

topCues: Initiate the roll-up from the abdominals, not by throwing the arms.
Keep the legs steady at a fixed angle as the torso moves.
Lengthen the spine into a long curve; avoid collapsing the chest.

name: Spine Stretch

breathShort.action: Sitting tall, nod the head and curl forward over the legs, drawing the navel back.
Restack the spine to sit tall, returning to the start.

topCues: Grow taller before you fold; create length, not a slump.
Keep the legs active and the heels reaching away.
Sit on top of the sit bones, not behind them.

name: Rolling Back

breathShort.action: Balance in a small ball shape and roll back to the shoulder blades.
Roll back up to the balance point without letting the feet touch.

topCues: Keep the shape tight and consistent; don't change the ball mid-roll.
Initiate the roll with the abdominals, not a head throw.
Roll along the spine smoothly, never onto the neck.

name: Seal

breathShort.action: Balance holding the ankles, then roll back to the shoulders.
Roll back up to balance, keeping the feet lifted.

topCues: Maintain the rounded shape throughout the roll.
Initiate from the deep abdominals, not momentum.
Keep the heels together and the elbows inside the knees.

name: Crab

breathShort.action: From a tight tucked shape, roll back onto the upper back.
Roll forward to balance, lengthening the spine, then re-tuck.

topCues: Keep the shape compact and controlled on the way back.
Protect the neck; roll only to the shoulder blades.
Use steady abdominal control rather than speed.

name: Rocker With Open Legs

breathShort.action: Balance holding the ankles with the legs open in a V, then roll back to the shoulders.
Roll back up to the balance point and hold steady.

topCues: Keep the arms and legs straight and the chest open.
Find the balance before rolling: control over momentum.
Keep the same V-shape throughout the roll.

name: Rollover With Legs Spread

breathShort.action: Lift the legs overhead, beginning to take the hips off the mat.
Roll the legs over until they are parallel to the floor behind you, then open them.
Begin to roll the spine back down with the legs spread.
Articulate the spine to the mat and lower the legs to the start.

topCues: Lift the hips with the abdominals, not by flinging the legs.
Keep the weight off the neck; stay on the shoulders.
Roll down one vertebra at a time with control.

name: Boomerang

breathShort.action: From a seated V with ankles crossed, roll back and overhead.
Change the cross of the ankles overhead.
Roll back up to balance in the teaser V.
Circle the arms back and clasp the hands behind you.
Fold forward over the legs with control.

topCues: Flow the transitions smoothly; it's one continuous motion.
Keep the legs at a steady height during the balance.
Control every phase from the center; avoid momentum.

name: Control Balance

breathShort.action: From overhead, split the legs and reach one toward the floor behind, holding the other up.
Switch the legs through center, keeping the hips lifted.

topCues: Keep the weight on the shoulders, never the neck.
Keep the pelvis steady through the leg change.
Reach long through both legs in opposite directions.

name: Jackknife

breathShort.action: Float the legs overhead, then lift them toward the ceiling, hips high.
Lower the spine and legs back down to the mat with control.

topCues: Lift from the abdominals, not by pushing the arms down.
Keep the weight off the neck and on the shoulders.
Articulate the spine down slowly on the return.

name: Shoulder Bridge

breathShort.action: Lift into a bridge and extend one leg toward the ceiling.
Lower and return the extended leg; keep the pelvis lifted and level.

topCues: Keep the hips level; don't let the lifted side drop.
Press evenly through the supporting foot and shoulders.
Stabilize through the hamstrings and deep abdominals.

name: Scissors

breathShort.action: Support the hips, legs to the ceiling, and split them front and back.
Switch the legs through center with a light pulse at the ends.

topCues: Keep the pelvis still and supported by the hands.
Reach long through both legs in opposite directions.
Stay lifted on the shoulders, off the neck.

name: Bicycle

breathShort.action: On the shoulders, split the legs and bend the back knee toward you.
Pedal the legs through, drawing the opposite knee in.
Continue the cycling motion smoothly.
Reverse or repeat, keeping the pelvis high and stable.

topCues: Keep the hips supported and lifted throughout.
Make the pedaling large but controlled.
Avoid weight on the neck; stay on the shoulders.

name: Leg Pull

breathShort.action: In a reverse plank, lift one leg toward the ceiling.
Lower the leg back down, keeping the hips lifted.
Lift the other leg with the same control.
Lower it, maintaining a long, level line.

topCues: Keep the hips high; don't let the seat sink.
Press through the shoulders and keep the chest open.
Move only the leg; the torso stays steady.

name: Leg Pull Front

breathShort.action: In a front plank, lift one leg, flexing the standing ankle forward.
Lower the leg and shift back over the heel.
Lift the other leg, keeping the torso level.
Lower it, returning to a strong plank line.

topCues: Keep the hips level and the shoulders over or near the wrists.
Lift the leg from the back of the hip without arching.
Maintain one long line from head to heels.

name: Push-Up

breathShort.action: Roll down through the spine and walk the hands out to a plank.
Bend and straighten the elbows close to the body, then walk back and roll up.

topCues: Keep the elbows hugging the ribs as you bend.
Hold a steady plank: no sagging or piking.
Articulate the spine on the way down and up.

name: Side Kick

breathShort.action: Lying on the side, sweep the top leg forward in two controlled beats.
Sweep the leg back behind the body, lengthening the front of the hip.

topCues: Hold the torso still and keep length under the bottom waist.
Move the leg at hip height with stable hips.
Stabilize through the deep abdominals, not momentum.

name: Side Kick Kneeling

breathShort.action: From a kneeling side support, lift the top leg to hip height and sweep it forward.
Sweep the leg back, keeping the torso steady over the supporting arm.

topCues: Keep a straight line from knee to head; don't sag.
Move the leg without rocking the pelvis.
Press firmly through the supporting hand.

name: Side Bend

breathShort.action: From a side-sit, press up into a side plank, lifting the hips high.
Lower the hips back down with control to the start.

topCues: Lift the hips toward the ceiling: make a long arc.
Stack the shoulder over the supporting hand.
Keep the body in one plane, not rolling forward or back.

name: Spine Twist

breathShort.action: Sitting tall, rotate the torso to one side in two pulses.
Return to center, growing taller.
Rotate to the other side with two controlled pulses.
Return to center with length.

topCues: Keep the hips square and grounded as you turn.
Rotate around a long, lifted spine.
Keep the arms in line with the shoulders.

name: Saw

breathShort.action: Sit tall and rotate the torso toward one leg, arms reaching wide.
Fold forward and reach the back hand past the opposite foot.
Roll up to sit tall and return through center.
Rotate and fold to the other side with the same reach.

topCues: Keep both sit bones grounded as you twist.
Twist first, then fold; lengthen before reaching.
Reach the opposite hand back to deepen the wring-out.

name: Twist

breathShort.action: From a side plank, lift the hips high.
Thread the top arm under the body, twisting the torso.
Unwind and reach the arm back up and over.
Lower back through the side plank to the start.

topCues: Keep the hips lifted throughout the twist.
Rotate from the waist, following the moving arm with the eyes.
Press firmly through the supporting hand and keep the shoulder stable.

name: Corkscrew

breathShort.action: With the legs together and lifted, sweep them to one side.
Circle the legs down and around through center.
Sweep up the other side, controlling the hips.
Return the legs to the top, pelvis stable.

topCues: Glue the legs together and move them as a single unit.
Anchor the upper back and shoulders to the mat.
Control the circle with the abdominals, not momentum.

name: Hip Twist With Stretched Arms

breathShort.action: Balance in a V with the hands behind you, sweep the straight legs to one side and down.
Circle the legs around through the bottom.
Sweep up the other side, keeping the chest lifted.
Return the legs to the top center.

topCues: Keep the chest open and the torso still as the legs circle.
Press the hands down for a stable base.
Make the circles even and controlled in both directions.

name: Cat Stretch

breathShort.action: On all fours, round the spine, drawing the navel up toward the ceiling.
Return through a long, neutral spine.
Continue articulating, pressing the floor away.
Lengthen the spine forward to neutral.

topCues: Move the spine one segment at a time, like a wave.
Keep the shoulders away from the ears and the elbows soft.
Coordinate the breath with the spinal motion.

name: One-Leg Kick

breathShort.action: Propped on the forearms, lengthen the spine and lift the chest.
Pulse one heel toward the seat twice, then switch legs.

topCues: Hold the chest high while keeping the pubic bone grounded.
Keep the thighs and knees together.
Lengthen the lower back rather than compressing it.

name: Double Kick

breathShort.action: Head turned to one side, hands clasped on the back, kick both heels to the seat three times.
Extend the legs and lift the chest, reaching the clasped hands toward the feet.

topCues: Keep the kicks controlled with the knees together.
Open the chest on the extension without straining the neck.
Anchor the hips down into the mat as the chest rises.

name: Swimming

breathShort.action: Lying prone, lift the arms, legs, and chest, then flutter opposite arm and leg as you breathe in for a count.
Keep the flutter going steadily as you breathe out over the next count.

topCues: Keep the limbs long and the belly lifted off the mat.
Move briskly but keep the torso stable.
Reach in opposite directions rather than just lifting higher.

name: Rocking

breathShort.action: Hold the ankles, lift the chest and thighs, and rock forward onto the chest.
Rock back onto the thighs, keeping the legs pressing into the hands.

topCues: Drive the rocking by pressing the shins into the hands.
Keep the chest open and the gaze forward.
Maintain the bow shape throughout the rock.

name: Swan Dive

breathShort.action: From a low back extension, reach the chest forward and up into the swan.
Rock forward as the legs lift, reaching the arms long.
Rock back up to the extension, lengthening the spine.

topCues: Keep the legs active and lifted as the chest rises.
Initiate the dive from the upper back, not the low back.
Keep the motion rhythmic and controlled.
